sections.paragraph:
ἀνόνητος, ον (αν-priv., ὀνητός ‘profitable’, s. ὀνίναμαι; Soph., Pla. et al.; Mitt-Wilck. II/2, 88 IV, 9; PPrinc 119, 56 [IV a.d.]; Wsd 3:11; 4 Macc 16:7, 9; Jos., Bell. 1, 464, Vi. 422 al.) useless ἐπιθυμίαι ἀ. w. βλαβεραί 1 Ti 6:9 v.l. (for ἀνοήτους).—DELG s.v. ὀνίνημι.

priv. priv. = privativum (in grammar: privative, w. negating force)

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

PPrinc PPrinc = Papyri in the Princeton University Collections—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 86
